student_name: Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari
hub_subtitle: Inglês do dia a dia — apresentar-se, rotina e conversação

lesson.menu_title: The Language of Professional Identity -- Introducing Yourself as a Medical Professional in Transition
lesson.menu_desc: Introducing yourself as a professional in transition -- your field, your training, and where you are heading -- how to describe your career background and current situation in clear, natural English -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 19 -- The Language of Professional Identity
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 19 -- The Language of Professional Identity
lesson.grammar_point: describing your career background and current situation

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone, and welcome. Let me introduce myself. My name is Daniel Alves, and I am a doctor from Portugal. I studied medicine in Lisbon and I worked in a busy city hospital for almost ten years. Two years ago, I made a big transition and moved here to the United States. It has not been easy. I had to pass several exams to get my license, and I am still improving my English every day. Right now, I work as a resident, and I am learning how medicine works in a new system. My main field is family medicine, but I have recently started to specialize in care for older patients. My advice to all of you is simple: be patient with yourself. A career transition takes time, but your experience never disappears. Thank you, and I look forward to working with you.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sofia! I just wanted to share some good news with you. Do you remember I told you I wanted to change my career? Well, I finally did it. For fifteen years I worked as a nurse, but I always dreamed of teaching. So last year I went back to university and got a degree in education. It was a demanding year, honestly, and there were moments when I wanted to give up. But I have just finished my first month as a health teacher, and I love it. My nursing expertise helps me every single day in the classroom. I know you are going through your own transition with the medical exams, so I really wanted to tell you: it is worth it. Keep going. You have worked so hard, and you will get there. Call me soon and tell me how you are doing. Bye!

extra_audio.text: First, say your name and your field, so people know who you are. Then give your main qualification and where you studied. After that, say how long you have worked and what you specialize in. Next, explain your current situation or the transition you are going through. Finally, say what you are working toward now and what comes next.
